$comment: Structured-outputs schema for the AI judge's per-exercise response (Claude Messages API output_config.format). This is the model-facing subset of evaluation.json: verdict/summary/differences/bonus. points, failing_gate, failing_gate_detail, and hard_gates are computed/copied in Python (evaluator/ai_judge.py) — the model never sets them. Structured outputs forbid numeric min/max constraints, so points_deducted bounds are enforced in Python too.

verdict.description: 'fail' if any hard gate in the provided bundle failed, otherwise 'pass'.

summary.description: 1-3 factual sentences describing how the submission compares to the solution.

area.description: Short label for where the issue lives, e.g. 'Mapper configuration'.

description.description: What is different/wrong, naming every occurrence.

points_deducted.description: The literal value stated by the governing rule (0 for mention-only notes). Never invented.

rule_source.description: Which rule the deduction comes from, e.g. 'general_rules: filter before sort' or 'notes.md: <rule>'. Use 'none' for mention-only observations with no governing rule.

bonus_question_answer.description: Summary + correctness assessment of the student's bonus answer, or null when the exercise has no bonus question.
